Off Calendar Nov-24-2009 Off Calendar

Hearing On The Petitions To Approve The Compromises Of Two Minors.
Off Calendar Nov-24-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for hearing on the Petition to Approve the Compromise of a Disputed Claim of Two Minors.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: K. Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Douglas K. deVries, Esq. appeared with petioner Roderick G.S. Sanden, who was present as Guardian ad litem for the minors Abbie Maria Sanden and Annie Theresa Sanden, who were not present at today's hearing.
Petitioner was placed under oath and examined.

On the basis of evidence received, and good cause apparing, the Petition is granted and compromise is approved.

As to Abbie Maria Sanden:

Settlment Amount - $107,500.00.
Attorney Fees - $22,248,32,
Medical Expenses - $34,700.00.
Costs - $6,055.04.

The net balance of $44,496.64 shall be deposited into a blocked account with Bank of America, main Roseville branch, Douglas Boulevard, Roseville, CA. No withdrawals shall be made without a written Court Order, or until the minor reaches the age of 18 years.

As to Annie Theresa Sanden:

Settlment Amount - $107,500.00.
Attorney Fees - $22,248,32,
Medical Expenses - $34,700.00.
Costs - $6,055.04.

The net balance of $44,496.64 shall be deposited into a blocked account with Bank of America, main Roseville branch, Douglas Boulevard, Roseville, CA. No withdrawals shall be made without a written Court Order, or until the minor reaches the age of 18 years.

Orders Approving Compromise of the Disputed Claim of each Minor, Deposit Money Into Blocked Account, were submitted to and signed by the Court.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Jul-22-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS Abbie Maria Sanden and Annie Theresa Sanden, minors by and through their father and guardian ad litem Roderick G.S. Sanden, M.D. and Theresa M. Sanden MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: Douglas K. deVries, Esq. of deVries Law Firm (916) 473-4343, appearing for Plaintiff and moving party; Brad W. Seiling, Esq. of Manatt (310) 312-4234, appearing for Defendant Physicians' Service DBA Blue Shield and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for summary adjudication is denied as procedurally infirm. Pursuant to CCP section 437c(f)(1), a court can summarily adjudicate whether a duty is or is not owed. See Linden Partners vs. Wilshire Linden Associates (1998) 64 Cal.App. 4th 508, 522. Plaintiff's notice of motion seeks a ruling on Breach. The Court cannot grant the motion for summary adjudication as framed. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-22-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PHYSICIANS' SERVICE DBA BLUE SHIELD OF CALIFORNIA MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMM ADJUD.

4343, appearing for Plaint.Argued & the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for Summ Judgment/Summ Adjud is denied. The motion as to the breach of contract cause of action is denied as a matter of law. The Home Care Services Provision and Exclusion #21 are ambiguous and construed against dft. The motion is denied as to the bad faith and the claim for punitive damages because there is a triable issue of fact regarding whether dft. acted reasonably & with malice. (Order overruling all evid. objs. filed in open court & copies handed to counsel.) (Dr. Battin deposition 55:7-15; Deft's responses to plft's UMF #28-39. Dr. Battin assumed the problem were behavioral without much investigation.) The Court adds, as to the home benefits provision, it does not clearly state it will only cover home care from a licensed provider nor does it exclude intensive feeding therapy which is IFT. The contract refers to physical therapist with no license qualifying language. The policy does not have a quote, "plan meaning," that clearly excludes IFT. The ambiguity is not resolved by attempting to examine the insured objectively reasonable expectations. Therefore the provision is resolved against the insurer as a drafter of the policy. The IFT is covered. Re: the exclusion 21, exclusions in an insurance policy are strictly constructed against the insurer & liberally interpreted in favor of the insured. While the IFT has a behavioral component, it was also a service that addressed behavioral and psychological - physiological medical problems, citing the Patel deposition pages 64, 65.The ambiguity of whether "behavioral problems" exclusion applies to the IFT requires the doubt to be resolved in plaintiff's favor. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
